dialogue_line_replacements.text: Quiero que todos estudien el subjuntivo esta semana, ¿entendido?
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: I want everyone to study the subjunctive this week, understood?
Hopefully we'll have more classes like this! It's so good! (PR: está buenísima = colloquial praise)

cultural_notes_overrides.formality: In Puerto Rico, the subjunctive is used identically to Mexico in all formal and standard contexts. PR Spanish is known for its subject pronoun usage: '¿Quieres tú que yo lo haga?' is more common than in Mexico.
cultural_notes_overrides.regional_variations: Puerto Rican speakers use '¡Ojalá!' frequently in daily speech. '¡Ojalá que sí!' and '¡Ojalá que no!' are common reactions. The subjunctive after ojalá is standard across all PR registers.
